steps.title: Use custom regression
steps.content: For infinitely many solutions, the two sides must be identical for every $x$. A fast Desmos way to enforce that is to test two different $x$-values at once. Enter $x_1=[0,1]$, then regress the left side against the right side with $a(4x_1-8)+5a\sim4(a x_1-2a)-20$. Desmos returns $a=-4$.

steps.title: Verify algebraically
steps.content: Expanding confirms why the regression works. The left side is $a(4x-8)+5a=4ax-3a$. The right side is $4(ax-2a)-20=4ax-8a-20$. The $4ax$ terms already match, so the constants must match: $$-3a=-8a-20$$ $$5a=-20$$ $$a=-4$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A says $-4$ only. That is exactly what the regression and coefficient check found. Choice B gives $a=0$, which makes $0=-20$ after the $x$-terms cancel. Choice C is too broad because only one value works, and choice D is false because $a=-4$ does work. Choice A is correct.
